title: Intensely Smoky Kenyan Chicken Stew
description: A hearty and comforting Kenyan chicken stew with a smoky flavor, featuring peanut brittle and miso marinate, perfect for a sick day
instructions: In a large bowl, whisk together miso paste, peanut oil, smoked paprika, salt, and black pepper to make the marinade
Add the chicken to the marinade and mix well to coat, then cover and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes
Heat 1 tablespoon of peanut oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat
Remove the chicken from the marinade and cook until browned on all sides, about 5-7 minutes
Add the chopped onion to the pot and cook until softened, about 5 minutes
Add the minced garlic and grated ginger to the pot and cook for 1 minute
Add the chicken broth to the pot and bring to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 20 minutes
Stir in the crushed peanut brittle and cook for an additional 5 minutes
Serve the stew hot, garnished with chopped fresh herbs if desired
